milestone: A2 (A-b): in-place COW file insert - multi-block data extent
status: Passed (unit + host content match + Apple kernel md5 match + Apple fsck_apfs clean). Multi-block contiguous file insert certified.
capability: commitImageOnlyFileInsert writes a multi-block file as one contiguous data extent: it allocates N contiguous data blocks, writes the payload across them, and records a single j_file_extent (length = N blocks) + single j_phys_ext (block_count = N) record. A fail-closed guard (blocksAreContiguous) rejects fragmented free space (the future multi-extent case).
unit_test: tests/unit/test_partition_manager_core.cpp::apfsWriter_inPlaceFileInsertWritesMultiBlockExtent - inserts a 5000-byte file (two-block extent), asserts the reader size and that both contiguous data blocks (208, 209) hold the full payload.

vm_external_gate.environment: qemu/KVM macOS Sequoia recovery; apfs.kext 2332.101.1; in-place file-insert container with a 5000-byte (two-block) file (xid 3) attached raw as SATA disk0
method: 1. format-image (A2MB) -> commit-image-file-insert --file-name big.bin --payload-file (5000 random bytes), host md5 9a72661de2c243c9e6fed56aff43e345.
2. Apple kernel auto-mounted /Volumes/A2MB; `ls` shows big.bin 5000 bytes; diskutil unmount; fsck_apfs -n /dev/disk0 clean.
3. diskutil mount disk1s1; `md5 /Volumes/A2MB/big.bin` = 9a72661de2c243c9e6fed56aff43e345 - exact match with the host payload (Apple read both data blocks of the extent correctly).
vm_external_gate.kernel_data_read: md5 (/Volumes/A2MB/big.bin) = 9a72661de2c243c9e6fed56aff43e345 == host md5sum (byte-for-byte match across the two-block extent)
vm_external_gate.apple_fsck_apfs: fsroot tree / extent ref tree / volume object map space / allocated space all consistent; The volume A2MB appears to be OK; The container /dev/disk0 appears to be OK (no warnings).

gates: clang-format + lizard + cppcheck clean; commit 1156623 (all pre-commit hooks passed, no bypass).
